textPlain: I want to see a crypto-shortcircuit.

Crypto-Current (2021) — what a time capsule! That year felt like one giant, glorious experiment in making money, largely driven by digital art and meme coins. We learned that hype can move mountains, but we also learned that a bull market hides every single technical flaw.The real "current" wasn't the fleeting excitement; it was the slow realization that if crypto is going to genuinely matter, it can't just be about speculative collectibles. It has to be about utility. That’s why the industry is now hyper-focused on Real-World Assets (RWA)—using the blockchain to manage and trade actual commercial property, commodities, or institutional bonds. This is the grown-up phase of crypto.But here is the technical debt left over from 2021: you can’t move serious, regulated value when every major blockchain is a siloed system. That massive barrier is interoperability. We need ironclad, multi-chain standards and specialized tools to track, secure, and move these institutional-grade assets across the entire crypto landscape. That technical complexity is where most general platforms fail, but it's the exact gap that focused companies like Lumos Core are bridging (https://lumoscore.com/). 
The future isn't another hype cycle; it's robust, cross-chain RWA infrastructure.
